own_review_points: renderComparison: NOT_EQUAL / NOT_DISTINCT_FROM with a ModifiedSubQueryExpression rhs match neither inner branch and fall out of the if without the else, so NOTHING is rendered -> silent empty/invalid SQL (v8 caught the gap but mis-described it as falling through to renderComparisonEmulateIntersect)
resolveSqlTypeDescriptor: unrecognized ARRAY<...> component types (e.g. JSON) yield null sqlTypeCode and fall to super, which may not parse ARRAY<...> -> wrong type resolution
visitStandardTableUpdate appends ' and (' + whereFragment + ')'; if visitSpannerTableUpdate emitted no preceding WHERE this yields invalid SQL (set col=val and (...))
visitSpannerTableUpdate hardcodes alias suffix '1_0'; collision risk with multiple mutation targets
Dictionary.java drops quotes ('disc as "type"' -> 'disc as type'); 'type' reserved in SQL Server/HANA, testDiscriminator has no skip for those
getQuerySequencesString hardcodes '1 as increment' which may not match Spanner sequence semantics (start_with_counter/skip_range)
count registered with CountFunction(...,'||','string',false) - verify DISTINCT emulation matches Spanner
human-ref: CriteriaUpdateWithParametersTest:81 @SkipForDialect can now be removed (bug fixed in PR 12099) - file is past the truncation marker
notes: 94-file PR, small truncation (only 3k more chars; even the changed-files header line is cut). Most cited code is visible. v8 is strong: points 2 (ARRAY component fallthrough), 4 (NOT_EQUAL quantified subquery), 5 (reserved alias 'type'), 9 (sequence increment) are genuinely substantive. Point 4's mechanism is slightly mis-stated (it renders nothing rather than falling to emulateIntersect) but the conclusion is correct -> kept good not wrong. Points 7 (adapt-vs-skip) and 8 (feature-check is well-placed) are soft/praise -> trivial. Missed the human-ref skip-removal (out-of-window file) and the ' and (' invalid-SQL concern in visitStandardTableUpdate. No fabrications.
